title: anacamptis papilionacea subsp. grandiflora
intro: Anacamptis papilionacea subsp. grandiflora is a subspecies of orchid, commonly known as the fly orchid, characterized by its unique, butterfly-like shape. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making it useful for treating various health issues. The health benefits of Anacamptis papilionacea subsp. grandiflora include reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial and antifungal properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often dried and used in teas, infusions, or tinctures, although its use is largely based on folkloric evidence and requires further scientific research for confirmation.
